WALLA introduces a leave-one-out baseline into the net payout function, yielding three desirable properties: (1) dominant-strategy incentive compatibility of prediction under arbitrary belief structure, (2) advantage--wager alignment, where the optimal wager is proportional to the model's expected score advantage, and (3) prediction-agnostic wager optimization, enabling decentralized learning of wager policies without requiring optimal predictions. We further instantiate two mechanism variants that trade off normality and no-arbitrage while maintaining a bounded worst-case deficit for the mechanism. Experiments on question-answering and forecasting benchmarks across heterogeneous models and private-information settings show that WALLA matches centralized aggregation methods in predictive performance, while simultaneously achieving decentralized learning, advantage-aligned aggregation weights, uncertainty awareness, and incentive-compatible prediction.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为WALLA的激励兼容机制，通过让多个LLM报告预测和学习赌注，实现去中心化、鲁棒的聚合，同时满足优势-赌注对齐、预测激励兼容等理想性质。
AI.motivation: 在多LLM聚合的分散场景中，需要在不获取模型私有信息的情况下确定聚合权重，且权重需对策略性报告具有鲁棒性。
AI.method: 提出优势对齐赌注机制（WALLA），在净收益函数中引入留一法基线，使每个模型报告预测和学习赌注，以赌注作为权重聚合预测。
AI.result: 在问答和预测基准上，WALLA在预测性能上匹配集中式聚合方法，同时实现去中心化学习、优势对齐的聚合权重、不确定性感知和激励兼容的预测。
AI.conclusion: WALLA实现了三个理想性质：任意信念结构下的占优策略激励兼容、优势-赌注对齐、以及预测无关的赌注优化；还提供了两种权衡正态性与无套利性质的变体。

title: Conversational Human Audio-visual Talking Dialogue Generation
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2026 as a main paper
summary: Large-scale dyadic interactive audio-visual dialogue (DIAD) datasets provide fundamental data resources for developing humanoid interactive virtual agents and digital humans. However, collecting such data is time-consuming, expensive, and ethically sensitive. To address this, we propose CHAT, a new dyadic interactive audio-visual dialogue generation (DIADG) framework that generates diverse, paired, and mutually responsive speech-face dialogue clips from a single textual prompt. CHAT unifies large language models and talking face models with interactive audio and facial behaviour refinement modules, enabling the generation of aligned dyadic dialogue clips with diverse contents and facial identities. Experiments show that CHAT outperforms existing related methods designed for similar tasks under both objective and subjective evaluations. Moreover, our synthesised CHAT-AVD-50k dataset serves as effective pre-training data for downstream interactive head generation, consistently improving PerFRDiff and ReactDiff on REACT 2024. CHAT offers a scalable alternative to the costly and ethically sensitive collection of real dyadic interaction data.

AI.tldr: 提出CHAT框架，从单文本提示生成双人交互式音视频对话片段，无需真实数据采集，生成的数据能有效预训练下游交互式头部生成模型。
AI.motivation: 大规模双人交互式音视频对话数据集对开发人形交互虚拟代理至关重要，但采集此类数据耗时、昂贵且存在伦理敏感问题，因此需要一种可扩展的合成数据生成方法。
AI.method: 提出CHAT框架，统一大型语言模型和说话人脸模型，结合交互式音频和面部行为精炼模块，从单文本提示生成内容多样、身份可变的配对双人对话片段。
AI.result: CHAT在客观和主观评估中均优于现有方法；合成数据集CHAT-AVD-50k作为下游交互式头部生成的预训练数据，持续提升了PerFRDiff和ReactDiff在REACT 2024上的性能。
AI.conclusion: CHAT为昂贵的、伦理敏感的真实双人交互数据采集提供了一种可扩展的替代方案。

title: Graph-Aware Fuzzing for Graph Database Management Systems
summary: Graph Database Management Systems (GDBMSs) are essential infrastructure for managing interconnected data. Existing GDBMS testing methods primarily rely on differential and metamorphic testing. The result consistency oracles of these methods constrain inputs to queries that are comparable across engines or transformations, leaving single engine runtime failures, such as crashes and memory errors, insufficiently explored. Developing dedicated fuzzers for GDBMSs faces two key challenges: (1) generating valid and structurally diverse queries under complex graph constraints, and (2) guiding exploration to capture topology dependent execution behavior. To address these challenges, we propose GRAF, a black box fuzzing framework for GDBMS query engines. First, GRAF introduces graph context aware query generation based on cascading dependency resolution. It instantiates parameterized Cypher skeletons generated by a Large Language Model (LLM) by jointly resolving labels, relationship types, properties, values, and variable scopes against the active graph state. This process produces structurally diverse queries while eliminating syntactic and semantic violations. Second, GRAF applies five graph specific mutation operators guided by execution state feedback, including execution time, result size, and system status. This feedback steers exploration away from unproductive queries and expensive traversals, while prioritizing local mutations around abnormal executions. We evaluated GRAF against three existing approaches on six widely used GDBMSs. GRAF consistently improves line coverage by 31.6% to 41.1% over the strongest baseline on each target. In 12 hour fuzzing, it triggered 25 unique bugs, compared to six from all baselines combined. Overall, GRAF discovered 34 previously unknown bugs, with 32 confirmed by developers and 23 assigned CVEs.

AI.tldr: GRAF是一个针对图数据库管理系统（GDBMS）的黑盒模糊测试框架。它利用LLM生成Cypher查询骨架，通过级联依赖解析实现图上下文感知的查询生成，并引入五种图特定的变异算子，结合执行反馈引导探索。在6个主流GDBMS上的评估显示，GRAF将行覆盖率提升31.6%-41.1%，发现34个未知漏洞，其中32个已确认，23个获CVE编号。
AI.motivation: 现有GDBMS测试方法主要依赖差分测试和蜕变测试，这些方法的结果一致性预言限制了输入的可比性，导致单引擎运行时故障（如崩溃、内存错误）未被充分探索。专用模糊测试面临两大挑战：(1)在复杂图约束下生成有效且结构多样的查询，(2)引导探索以捕获依赖拓扑的执行行为。
AI.method: GRAF采用两阶段方法：(1)图上下文感知查询生成——利用LLM生成参数化Cypher骨架，通过级联依赖解析联合解析标签、关系类型、属性、值和变量作用域，生成结构多样的有效查询；(2)基于执行状态反馈的图特定变异——五种图变异算子结合执行时间、结果大小和系统状态反馈，避开低效查询和昂贵遍历，优先在异常执行周围进行局部变异。
AI.result: 在6个GDBMS上评估，GRAF相比最强基线一致提升31.6%-41.1%的行覆盖率。12小时模糊测试触发25个独特bug（基线共6个）。总计发现34个未知bug，32个获开发者确认，23个已分配CVE。
AI.conclusion: GRAF通过图上下文感知的查询生成和反馈引导的变异策略，有效解决了GDBMS模糊测试中的两大挑战，显著优于现有方法。结果表明，这种结合LLM生成与执行反馈的方法能高效发现图数据库引擎中的深层漏洞。

title: Risk-Constrained Freshness-Aware Semantic Caching for Open-Web Retrieval-Augmented LLMs
summary: Semantic caching reduces the latency and cost of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by serving cached answers to semantically similar queries, but most existing methods do not model the time-varying freshness of open-web evidence. We present FreshCache, a three-tier semantic cache that treats cache reuse as a risk-constrained temporal inference problem: before approving a cache hit, FreshCache estimates the probability that the cached result is stale using a fitted exponential decay model enhanced by a learned MLP, and approves reuse only when that probability falls below a per-tier error budget across answers (epsilon = 0.10), URL lists (epsilon = 0.20), and page content (epsilon = 0.35). This allows the system to degrade gracefully as entries age rather than forcing a binary choice between a stale hit and a full pipeline execution. We introduce FreshCache-Bench, a benchmark of 8,072 base queries across five freshness classes with ground truth staleness labels drawn from real web snapshots at 1, 12, 24 hours, and 7 days after a baseline crawl, expanded to 31,201 queries via paraphrase generation. At the 24-hour evaluation window, FreshCache_MLP achieves 97% search API savings at 0.1% hash-based stale error, and an LLM-judge evaluation on 396 confirmed change pairs shows that only 34.3% of detected content changes actually affect answer correctness, placing true answer-affecting stale error at approximately 0.034%. The rule-based FreshCache achieves 98% search savings at 3.3% stale error under a temporal holdout calibration, outperforming SemanticTTL (14.9% stale, 72% saved), vCache (7.2% stale, 47% saved), and SCALM (5.2% stale, 96% saved). Ablations show the temporal risk gate accounts for an 11.6 point reduction in stale error over similarity-only reuse, and the learned MLP reduces stale error a further 3.2 points over the rule-based model.

AI.tldr: FreshCache提出了一种三层语义缓存系统，通过指数衰减模型和MLP估计缓存结果过时的概率，并在误差预算控制下决定是否复用缓存，从而在RAG中实现低延迟、低成本且时效性感知的缓存管理。
AI.motivation: 现有语义缓存方法未考虑网络证据随时间的时效性变化，导致缓存结果可能过时。作者希望将缓存复用建模为风险约束的时间推理问题，实现从"二进制选择"到"优雅降级"的转变。
AI.method: 构建三层缓存（答案、URL列表、页面内容），每层设置不同误差预算（0.10/0.20/0.35）；使用指数衰减模型+MLP估计结果过时概率；仅在过时概率低于预算时批准缓存命中；并构建了FreshCache-Bench基准数据集。
AI.result: 24小时窗口下，FreshCache_MLP实现97%搜索API节省，仅0.1%哈希级过时错误；真正影响答案正确性的过时错误约0.034%；规则版FreshCache以3.3%过时错误实现98%搜索节省，显著优于SemanticTTL、vCache、SCALM等基线。
AI.conclusion: 时间风险门控相比仅靠语义相似性复用降低了11.6个百分点的过时错误，MLP进一步降低3.2个百分点。FreshCache在时效性与缓存利用率之间取得了更优的平衡。

title: MechMath Agent Team: LLM Driven Agents for Mathematical Research
summary: AI reasoning has become a central focus in contemporary artificial intelligence, largely driven by the success of large language models. However, mathematical research, which is characterized by non-linear derivation paths, rigorous logical requirements, and protracted exploration cycles, poses severe challenges for existing reasoning systems. To overcome these limitations, we present the MechMath Agent Team (MMAT), which is a large language model driven agent designed to serve as a co-pilot throughout the full cycle of mathematical research. We design a tripartite Harness Architecture that decouples system responsibilities into Control, Execution, and Augmentation planes, thereby reconciling rigorous logical control with the agility demanded by open-ended research. Building upon this framework, we instantiate three specialized agents: a Knowledge Base Manager, a Natural Language Prover, and a Formal Language Prover, all operating in a closed loop to produce formally certified mathematical proofs. We evaluate MMAT on open problems in Number Theory, Algebraic Complexity Theory, Differential Algebra, Operator Algebra, and Inequalities. Across a two-month deployment, 11 problems have been solved, demonstrating its capacity to act as a co-pilot throughout the entire research cycle. The contributions are threefold: a general decoupled Harness Architecture for multi-agent mathematical reasoning, its concrete instantiation in the MMAT system, and empirical validation on a diverse suite of open problems.

AI.tldr: 提出MechMath Agent Team (MMAT)，一种基于大语言模型的多智能体系统，通过解耦的Harness架构（控制、执行、增强三层）协调三个专门智能体（知识库管理器、自然语言证明器、形式语言证明器），在两个月内解决了数论、代数复杂性理论等领域的11个开放数学问题。
AI.motivation: 现有推理系统难以应对数学研究的非线性推导路径、严格的逻辑要求和漫长的探索周期，亟需一种能够覆盖数学研究全周期的智能辅助系统。
AI.method: 设计了三分量Harness架构，将系统职责解耦为控制平面、执行平面和增强平面；基于此框架实例化三个专门智能体（知识库管理器、自然语言证明器、形式语言证明器），形成闭环以产生形式化验证的数学证明。
AI.result: 在数论、代数复杂性理论、微分代数、算子代数和不等式领域的开放问题上进行为期两个月的部署，共解决11个问题。
AI.conclusion: 贡献在于：通用解耦的Harness多智能体数学推理架构、MMAT系统的具体实现、以及在多样化开放问题上的实证验证，证明了其作为数学研究全周期辅助伙伴的能力。

title: Prior Bias in Vision Language Models on UML Diagram Interpretation
summary: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly applied to software engineering artifacts, especially UML class diagrams whose meaning depends on visual notation. Yet, it is unclear whether VLMs actually read such diagrams or instead answer from pretrained priors about how classes typically relate. We introduce a controlled UML benchmark in which each prior-conforming diagram is paired with its prior-conflicting counterpart that (1) preserves the same class names and layout while (2) reverses only the relation arrow. We evaluate eight open-source VLMs from two model families, InternVL3.5 and Qwen3, alongside two closed-source frontier models GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4 Mini. Across the eight open-source models, reversing the arrow reduces relation-direction accuracy by 33.48% on average, while GPT-5.4 Mini retains a 10% gap. In the harder three-class condition, accuracy drops sharply by 45.28% for open-source models, and even 18.62% for the GPT-5.4 family on average. Scaling provides only limited improvements and is family-dependent. Our benchmark presents a diagnostic prior-driven failure in diagram-grounded software understanding. Our artifact is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/UMLKnowledgeConflict-8461.

AI.tldr: 该论文通过构建冲突性UML类图基准测试，发现视觉语言模型（VLMs）在理解类图时严重依赖预训练先验知识而非实际视觉符号，导致方向反转后关系判断准确率大幅下降（开源模型平均降33.48%，三分类场景降45.28%），且模型扩展只能带来有限且依赖模型家族的改进。
AI.motivation: 当前视觉语言模型广泛应用于软件工程制品（尤其是UML类图），但这类制品含义依赖于视觉符号，然而尚不清楚VLM是真正读懂类图，还是仅依据类之间如何关联的预训练先验知识作答。为此，研究设计了一个控制性基准来诊断这一先验驱动问题。
AI.method: 1) 构建成对UML类图基准：标准图（符合先验知识）与冲突图（保持相同类名和布局，仅反转关系箭头方向）；2) 评估8个开源VLM（InternVL3.5和Qwen3系列）和2个闭源前沿模型（GPT-5.4及GPT-5.4 Mini）；3) 比较箭头反转前后的关系方向判断准确率差异。
AI.result: 1) 箭头反转后，开源模型平均关系方向准确率下降33.48%；2) GPT-5.4 Mini仍存在10%的准确率差距；3) 更困难的三类条件下，开源模型准确率骤降45.28%，GPT-5.4系列平均下降18.62%；4) 模型规模扩展带来的改进有限且依赖于模型家族。
AI.conclusion: 当前VLM在理解UML类图时存在严重的先验驱动失败问题（prior-driven failure），模型更依赖预训练知识而非实际视觉符号进行推理，即使扩展模型规模也无法有效解决这一根本性缺陷。基准测试和代码已开源。

title: EvoEye: Self-Evolving Runtime Monitoring for Autonomous Driving Systems
summary: Runtime monitoring is essential for detecting impending hazards in autonomous driving systems (ADSs). However, existing ADS runtime monitors have fixed detection capabilities: rule-based monitors cover only manually specified hazards, while learning-based monitors depend heavily on their initial training data and may retain substantial prediction errors. We therefore propose EvoEye, which identifies the current monitor's errors, generates informative executions accordingly, and updates the monitor through self-evolution. To enable effective self-evolution, EvoEye combines a capable runtime monitor with targeted scenario acquisition. FusionMonitor learns cross-module temporal interactions for collision prediction, while BlindSpotEvolver converts current prediction errors into search guidance and uses density-aware mutation to acquire informative executions for subsequent monitor updates. We evaluate EvoEye on Baidu Apollo with CARLA in representative highway and urban scenarios. FusionMonitor improves frame-level Recall by up to 37.8 percentage points at a false positive rate of 0.05, with 2.49 ms latency and 2.8-4.2 seconds of median warning time. Under the same budget, BlindSpotEvolver outperforms uniform and violation-oriented sampling by up to 13.2 F1 points on previously missed unsafe contexts.

AI.tldr: EvoEye提出了一种自我进化的ADS运行时监控框架，通过识别当前监控器的错误、生成有信息的执行场景并更新监控器，显著提升了碰撞预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有ADS运行时监控器检测能力固定：基于规则的监控器仅覆盖手动指定的危险，基于学习的监控器严重依赖初始训练数据且存在较大预测误差。
AI.method: EvoEye包含两个核心组件：FusionMonitor（学习跨模块时序交互进行碰撞预测）和BlindSpotEvolver（将当前预测错误转化为搜索引导，使用密度感知变异获取有信息的执行场景用于监控器更新）。
AI.result: 在百度Apollo和CARLA评估中，FusionMonitor在0.05假阳性率下将帧级召回率提升最多37.8个百分点，延迟2.49ms，中位预警时间2.8-4.2秒；BlindSpotEvolver在同等预算下比均匀采样和面向违规采样在遗漏不安全场景上提升最多13.2 F1分。
AI.conclusion: EvoEye通过自我进化机制有效克服了现有监控器的固定检测能力限制，在多种场景下显著提升了ADS运行时监控的准确性和及时性。

title: CausalGame: Benchmarking Causal Thinking of LLM Agents in Games
comment: Zhenhao, Yongqiang, and Chenxi contributed equally to the project. A short version is accepted at the Forty-Third International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2026 as an Oral presentation. Project website https://causalgame.github.io/
summary: Building AI Scientist agents with Large Language Models (LLMs) has recently attracted growing attention. Since scientific discovery fundamentally relies on uncovering causal relationships from observations, the capability of causal thinking, i.e., distinguishing causation from correlation and recognizing hidden biases, is essential to LLM agents. Although a number of benchmarks exist for AI Scientists, none explicitly incorporate challenges from selection bias, measurement error, and hidden confounders that widely exist in real-world scientific discovery. To this end, we present CausalGame, a benchmark that evaluates the causal thinking capabilities of LLM agents through interactive games. CausalGame asks LLM agents to actively design experimental protocols, collect observation data, and derive a final solution with an explanation report. To emulate realistic scientific discovery challenges, we design 14 scenarios that incorporate selection bias, measurement error, and hidden confounders. Across 30 LLM agents, none demonstrates reliable causal thinking: the best model reaches only 68.0% survival against analytical optima of 78-85%, and merely 5-7% of sessions receive credits on the causal-reasoning rubrics. CausalGame provides a scalable and controlled testbed for evaluating the causal thinking of AI Scientist agents.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了CausalGame基准，通过14个包含选择偏差、测量误差和隐藏混杂因素的交互式游戏场景，评估LLM代理的因果思维能力。实验表明，30个LLM代理中没有一个表现出可靠的因果思维，最佳模型仅达到68%的生存率（最优78-85%），且仅5-7%的会话在因果推理评分中获得分数。
AI.motivation: 现有AI科学家基准未涵盖现实科学发现中普遍存在的选择偏差、测量误差和隐藏混杂因素，缺乏专门评估LLM代理因果思维能力（区分因果关系与相关性、识别隐藏偏见）的基准。
AI.method: 设计了14个交互式游戏场景，要求LLM代理主动设计实验方案、收集观测数据，并给出最终解决方案及解释报告。场景中系统性地融入了选择偏差、测量误差和隐藏混杂因素这三大科学发现中的典型挑战。
AI.result: 测试了30个LLM代理，最佳模型（如DeepSeek-R1）仅达到68%的生存率，远低于最优分析解78-85%；仅有5-7%的会话在因果推理评分中获得分数，所有代理均未展现可靠的因果思维能力。
AI.conclusion: CausalGame提供了一个可扩展、可控的测试平台，能够有效评估AI科学家代理的因果思维能力。当前LLM代理在因果推理方面存在显著不足，亟需改进。

title: LLM-as-a-Tutor: Policy-Aware Prompt Adaptation for Non-Verifiable RL
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) for non-verifiable instruction following increasingly relies on LLM judges with prompt-specific rubrics as reward signals. While recent methods adapt these rubrics to the evolving policy during training, the training prompts themselves remain static, drawn from fixed corpora. This static approach often results in a critical misalignment between prompt difficulty and policy capability, leaving the judge unable to recover a discriminative reward signal when prompts fail to elicit quality variance among rollouts. To address this misalignment, we introduce LLM-as-a-Tutor, a framework that extends the LLM's role from judge to tutor: a single model serves as an examiner that pairwise-compares policy rollouts to detect non-challenging prompts, and as a generator that appends atomic constraints to them. This append-only design monotonically raises difficulty in step with the policy's capability, producing a self-calibrating training signal without external difficulty schedules. On three complex instruction-following benchmarks, our method consistently outperforms both policy-unaware baselines and prior policy-adaptive methods that adapt rubrics or rewrite prompts, suggesting prompt adaptation as a missing axis of policy-awareness in non-verifiable RL.

AI.tldr: 本文提出LLM-as-a-Tutor框架，将LLM从裁判扩展为导师角色，通过检测非挑战性提示并追加原子约束，动态调整提示难度以匹配策略能力，在三个复杂指令遵循基准上优于现有方法。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习方法中，训练提示保持静态，导致提示难度与策略能力错位，当提示无法引发rollout间的质量差异时，判别性奖励信号无法恢复。
AI.method: 提出LLM-as-a-Tutor框架：单一LLM同时充当考官（成对比较策略rollout以检测非挑战性提示）和生成器（向非挑战性提示追加原子约束），通过只追加不修改的设计单调提升难度，形成自校准训练信号。
AI.result: 在三个复杂指令遵循基准上，该方法一致优于不考虑策略的基线和先前的策略自适应方法。
AI.conclusion: 提示自适应是策略感知强化学习中缺失的一个重要维度，LLM-as-a-Tutor通过动态调整提示难度有效解决了奖励信号退化问题。

title: Cancelable Biometric Template Protection Based on Multi-Instance Fusion: A Contralateral Iris Approach
summary: Biometric templates are vulnerable to theft if stored without protection. Unlike passwords, a compromised iris cannot be reissued. Although existing cancelable biometric schemes address this problem, most still require an external key or token, introducing an additional attack surface. This paper proposes a cancelable contralateral iris template protection scheme that eliminates the need for a separate token or stored secret, satisfying the three requirements of ISO/IEC 24745: irreversibility, unlinkability, and confidentiality. The method fuses three enrollment samples per eye using Majority Vote Fusion to produce a stable template, and applies a salt-based bit permutation derived from the subject's enrollment ID. Combining the left- and right-permuted templates via a bitwise XOR produces a single Protected Fused Template. Since left and right iris patterns are statistically independent, fusing contralateral irises improves the accuracy of the system. An attacker must possess both iris codes and both salts to recover any useful information, yielding a larger effective key space than single-iris schemes. Experiments on three datasets, CASIA-IrisV4-Interval, CASIA-IrisV2 (two devices), and CASIA-Iris-Thousand, yield EERs of $0.36$\%, $4.88$\%, $10.80$\%, and $3.35$\%, respectively; the highest value reflects the more challenging cross-device scenario. These results demonstrate that our contralateral approach outperforms unprotected baselines while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art cancelable methods. %In addition, an ablation study confirms the benefit on both recognition performance and security. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first scheme to combine tokenless multi-instance fusion and contralateral binding under ISO/IEC 24745.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无外部令牌的抵消性双虹膜模板保护方案，融合左右虹膜并通过ID衍生盐值进行比特置换与异或操作，满足ISO/IEC 24745标准，实验表明优于未保护基线且与先进方法竞争力相当。
AI.motivation: 现有可取消生物特征方案大多需要外部密钥或令牌，增加了攻击面；而虹膜模板一旦泄露无法像密码一样重新签发，亟需一种无外部令牌、同时满足ISO/IEC 24745三项要求（不可逆性、不可链接性、机密性）的方案。
AI.method: 1）对每只眼睛的三个注册样本进行多数投票融合生成稳定模板；2）基于用户注册ID生成盐值，对左右虹膜模板分别进行比特置换；3）将置换后的左右模板进行按位异或（XOR），生成单一的保护融合模板。由于左右虹膜统计独立，融合提高了识别精度。
AI.result: 在CASIA-IrisV4-Interval、CASIA-IrisV2（两个设备）、CASIA-Iris-Thousand四个场景下的EER分别为0.36%、4.88%、10.80%和3.35%，最高值对应更困难的跨设备场景。性能优于未保护基线，与现有可取消方法竞争力相当。
AI.conclusion: 该方案是首个将无令牌多实例融合与对侧绑定结合起来并满足ISO/IEC 24745标准的方法，在安全性和识别性能上均有优势，攻击者需同时获取两个虹膜码和两个盐值才能恢复信息，有效密钥空间更大。

title: Semantic-aware and Self-improving Program Reduction via Agentic Large Language Models
summary:
Reducing bug-triggering programs to their minimal essential form is a fundamental task in debugging language processors such as compilers and interpreters. Existing reduction techniques are limited by their reliance on predefined, syntax-driven transformations that lack semantic understanding of the target program, and by their inability to learn from past reduction experiences.
  We present a new approach that recasts program reduction as an autonomous reasoning task powered by agentic Large Language Models (LLMs). Instead of applying fixed transformation rules, our method enables an LLM to analyze program semantics, formulate reduction hypotheses, and iteratively refine its approach based on execution outcomes. Successful reduction experiences are further distilled into reusable strategies, allowing the system to continuously improve over time.
  We realize this approach in PROJ, a framework built around two collaborative components: a reducer agent that performs semantic-aware, case-specific program reduction, and a reflector agent that extracts and accumulates transferable reduction knowledge. Extensive experiments on 90 benchmarks spanning three programming languages show that PROJ consistently produces smaller reduced programs than all existing state-of-the-art reducers while maintaining high efficiency.

AI.tldr: 本文提出PROJ框架，利用大语言模型作为智能体，通过语义理解和经验积累实现比现有方法更精简的程序化简。
AI.motivation: 现有程序化简技术依赖预定义的语法驱动转换规则，缺乏对目标程序的语义理解，且无法从过去的化简经验中学习。
AI.method: 将程序化简重构为自主推理任务：reducer agent负责语义感知的针对性化简，reflector agent负责提取和积累可迁移的化简知识，两者协同工作。
AI.result: 在涵盖三种编程语言的90个基准测试上，PROJ始终能生成比所有现有最先进化简工具更小的简化程序，同时保持高效率。
AI.conclusion: 基于LLM智能体的语义感知方法能突破传统语法驱动化简的局限，且通过知识蒸馏实现持续改进，为程序化简任务提供了新的范式。

title: Legible-by-Construction: Attention and End-to-End Transformers
summary: A companion paper showed that a transformer's feed-forward layer can be rebuilt from explicit fuzzy set operations - intersection, set-difference, and a self-forgetting sequence quantifier - so its hidden units read as named logical operators at no cost to language-model quality. That left the other half of the transformer opaque. Here we carry the same idea into attention and join the two into one model. The mechanism is minimal: a head's value is passed through a sigmoid, so each value channel becomes a readable detector of whether a feature holds at a token. This adds no parameters and leaves the standard head otherwise untouched. A Boolean variant goes further, restructuring the value into an explicit within-token intersection and negation-capable set-difference. In both designs the output projection is left free, not tied to the vocabulary, which is the load-bearing decision: bounding what a head detects while leaving what it writes unconstrained yields selective detectors, whereas constraining the write does not. A bounded value is shaped into a readable detector by two selectivity pressures - one for sparse firing, one for decisive firing at the rails - and which a design wants is not universal. Across five specialized-attention designs at 125M parameters, 44 to 62 percent of value channels become crisp, contextually selective detectors, and their legibility rises with depth rather than crystallizing only on punctuation. Language-model quality is at parity with a conventional baseline. Finally, we couple the Boolean attention to the legible feed-forward layer and train an end-to-end legible-by-construction language model at benchmark parity: its feed-forward units are named set and quantifier operations throughout, and we can take a token it generates and read the named units that compose to produce it.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可解释的注意力机制，通过对注意力头的值进行sigmoid约束或布尔重构，使得每个值通道成为可读的特征检测器，并与已可解释的前馈层结合，构建了端到端可解释的语言模型，在保持基准性能的同时实现了模型内部机制的透明化。
AI.motivation: 前馈层已通过模糊集合论操作（交、差、自遗忘序列量词）实现可解释构建，但注意力层仍是黑箱。本文旨在将同样的可解释性思路引入注意力机制，最终实现完整的端到端可解释语言模型。
AI.method: 1) 设计最小化机制：对注意力头的值通过sigmoid激活，使每个值通道成为"特征是否存在"的可读检测器，不增加参数；2) 设计布尔变体：将值重构为显式的令牌内交集和可执行差集操作；3) 关键决策：输出投影保持自由（不绑定到词汇表），边界检测（读）而非约束写入（写），以产生选择性检测器；4) 通过两种选择性压力（稀疏激活和决定性激活）塑造可读检测器。
AI.result: 在5种专门注意力设计（125M参数）中，44%-62%的值通道成为清晰、上下文选择性的检测器，且可读性随深度增加而提升（而非仅集中在标点符号）。语言模型质量与常规基线持平。最终将布尔注意力与可读前馈层耦合，训练出端到端可解释语言模型，其前馈单元全程为命名的集合和量词操作，可读取生成令牌背后组合的命名单元。
AI.conclusion: 通过约束注意力头的"检测"（读）而非"写入"（写），可以在不牺牲模型质量的前提下实现注意力层的可解释性；两种选择性压力（稀疏vs决定性）各有适用场景，不存在普适最优；最终成功构建了完整的、端到端可解释的语言模型，为深度学习模型的可解释性提供了新的范式。

title: Agent Step Value: State-Transition Measurement with State-Grounded LLM Evaluators
summary: Most agent evaluations collapse a multi-step trace into a final answer, a success flag, or a trajectory-level score. These aggregates obscure the diagnostic question developers need most: which action changed the state in a useful direction? We introduce Agent Step Value (ASV), a state-transition measurement framework that scores each observed action by the change it induces in a state-grounded evaluator's distribution over fixed candidate outcomes. ASV renders redacted before/after state projections, uses a stateless LLM evaluator to assign candidate log scores, and reports both gold-free belief diagnostics and offline oracle validation metrics. A label-free rationale pass separates evaluator deliberation from one-token option scoring, preserving candidate likelihoods while exposing leakage and floor-score events. On 100 reviewed open-QA evidence-seeking tasks with live PubMed retrieval, a partially live DeepSeek actor, and DeepSeek log-probability scoring, ASV evaluates 1,100 steps and 2,200 states. Under the fixed-layout rationale-conditioned protocol, mean gold-margin gain is -2.335 (trajectory-bootstrap 95\% CI [-3.395, -1.272]), entropy movement is 0.000, and mean Bayesian surprise is 2.693. ASV therefore localizes constructive and destructive belief pivots that final-answer scores and entropy-only step metrics miss. We release the standalone ASV Eval toolkit.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Agent Step Value (ASV)，一种基于状态转换的智能体步骤评估框架，通过测量每步动作引起的状态变化来诊断智能体行为的有用性，弥补了传统聚合评分无法定位具体行动贡献的缺陷。
AI.motivation: 现有智能体评估方法将多步轨迹压缩为最终答案或轨迹级评分，掩盖了开发者最需要的诊断信息——哪一步动作在有用方向上改变了状态。需要一种能够逐步骤定位动作价值的评估方法。
AI.method: ASV框架：(1) 对前后状态进行脱敏投影；(2) 使用无状态LLM评估器分配候选结果的对数分数；(3) 提供无标签信念诊断和离线Oracle验证指标；(4) 通过无标签理由通道分离评估器推理与选项评分，保留候选似然并暴露泄露和地板事件。
AI.result: 在100个开放QA证据检索任务、部分在线DeepSeek执行器和DeepSeek对数概率评分下，评估1100步和2200个状态。固定布局理由条件的协议下：平均黄金边际增益为-2.335，熵移为0.000，平均贝叶斯惊喜为2.693。
AI.conclusion: ASV能够定位最终答案分数和纯熵指标无法捕捉的建设性与破坏性信念转变，为智能体行为诊断提供了更细粒度的评估工具。

title: E-TraMamba: A New Paradigm for Efficient Long-Term 3D Feature Tracking with Event Cameras
summary: Event-based 3D tracking enables low-latency and high-speed perception, while existing CNN- and Transformer-based trackers struggle to capture long-range spatiotemporal dependencies in sparse, noisy event streams, especially under real-time and efficiency constraints. To address these challenges, we present E-TraMamba, the first Mamba-based framework for 3D feature tracking on event data. This new framework adopts a linear state-space model for efficient long-range modeling and integrates a lightweight affine-transform predictor to maintain stable tracking under motion blur and occlusion. We also design an effective scheme to fuse multi-scale cues -- local spatiotemporal patches, correlation maps, and positional embeddings -- into a unified representation that enables stable and smooth 3D tracking. We construct a large-scale synthetic dataset, named EvD-PointOdyssey, which is generated with monocular rendering and provides synchronized event streams, depth maps, and accurate 3D trajectories for training and evaluating event-based 3D tracking models. Extensive experiments on event-based benchmarks demonstrate that E-TraMamba achieves state-of-the-art performance, delivering over $2\times$ longer feature lifetimes under strict accuracy thresholds (e.g., 0.1 m), with higher tracked-feature ratios and lower RMSE than all baselines. These results make E-TraMamba a strong candidate for low-latency visual odometry, real-time SLAM, and interactive robotics.

AI.tldr: E-TraMamba是首个基于Mamba的3D事件特征跟踪框架，利用线性状态空间模型实现高效长程建模，在事件流数据上达到SOTA性能，特征寿命延长2倍以上，适用于低延迟视觉里程计和实时SLAM。
AI.motivation: 现有CNN和Transformer跟踪器难以在稀疏、嘈杂的事件流中捕捉长程时空依赖关系，且受限于实时性和效率约束。
AI.method: 提出Mamba框架（线性状态空间模型），结合轻量级仿射变换预测器，设计多尺度融合方案（局部时空块、相关图、位置嵌入），并构建合成数据集EvD-PointOdyssey。
AI.result: 在严格精度阈值（0.1m）下特征寿命延长2倍以上，跟踪特征比率更高，RMSE更低，在事件基准上达到SOTA。
AI.conclusion: E-TraMamba为低延迟视觉里程计、实时SLAM和交互式机器人提供了强有力的候选方案。

title: SkillFab: An Agent-Native Skill Production Platform
summary:
SkillFab is an agent-native platform for turning missing capabilities into reviewed, reusable Agent Skills. At runtime, agents first search for reusable skills; when no adequate skill exists, the unmet capability becomes a demand-first issue before any repository or implementation branch needs to exist. Development then proceeds through a SkillFab-managed repository, Git-ingested commit evidence, maintainer review, and registry publication. The same lifecycle is exposed through web, REST, and MCP surfaces, so humans, scripts, and external agents operate on shared state rather than separate task logs. The current system uses scoped Git push URLs, native range commit ingestion, workflow-state reads, and workflow-event histories to make long-running agent work reviewable and recoverable. We document the platform model, architecture, implemented capabilities, and three case studies: an end-to-end OS-detect skill run, a Docker research package that converts operational practice into reusable skill knowledge, and an external optimization case showing how improved skill artifacts can enter SkillFab as reviewable, versioned submissions.
  Deployment: https://skillfab.ai.

AI.tldr: SkillFab是一个面向Agent的原生平台，用于将缺失的能力转化为可审查、可复用的Agent技能。它通过需求驱动、Git版本管理、审核发布机制，实现从需求发现到技能注册的全生命周期管理，并通过Web、REST、MCP多接口暴露，支持人、脚本和外部Agent协同操作。
AI.motivation: Agent在执行任务时常遇到能力缺失问题，现有方案缺乏标准化的技能发现、开发、审核和复用的全生命周期管理机制，导致重复开发、质量不可控、协作困难。
AI.method: 采用"需求优先"策略，运行时先搜索可复用技能；无合适技能时触发需求记录，再通过Git仓库管理、提交证据审查、维护者审核、注册表发布的流程推进。系统通过Web、REST和MCP接口暴露统一生命周期，使用Git推送URL、范围提交提取、工作流状态读取和事件历史记录来支持可审查和可恢复的长期Agent任务。
AI.result: 实现了端到端OS检测技能运行、Docker研究包将运维实践转化为可复用技能知识、以及外部优化案例展示改进后的技能工件如何以可审查、版本化的提交形式进入SkillFab。
AI.conclusion: SkillFab通过需求驱动的技能开发流程和统一的协作接口，有效解决了Agent技能缺失时的标准化复用问题，提升了技能开发的可审查性、可恢复性和协作效率。

title: WPG-MoE: Weak-Prior-Guided Dense Mixture-of-Experts for User-Level Social Media Depression Detection
summary: Online social media posts provide scalable signals for early depression screening, and recent studies mainly improve pre-classification evidence through risk-post selection, symptom grounding, and clinically informed feature construction. However, these screening-stage designs often leave final decisions to a single detector, overlooking how users heterogeneously express depressive risk after screening. A monolithic classifier must average across heterogeneous users, which may dilute localized evidence and cause misclassification, especially for non-self-disclosing users. To address this issue, we propose WPG-MoE, a weak-prior-guided dense mixture-of-experts framework built on a shared large language model (LLM) backbone. WPG-MoE derives user-level weak semantic priors to softly route users to experts matched to different evidence layouts. We formulate this process as learning using privileged information (LUPI): rich LLM-extracted structured evidence guides training-time routing, while inference retains only Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) template screening and the deployable backbone. Experiments on Chinese and English datasets show that WPG-MoE outperforms strong baselines with interpretable routing behavior.

AI.tldr: 针对单分类器忽略用户异质性表达的问题，提出WPG-MoE框架，利用弱语义先验将不同证据布局的用户路由到匹配的专家模型，在中文和英文数据集上取得最优效果。
AI.motivation: 现有抑郁筛查方法在筛查后使用单一分类器进行最终决策，但用户对抑郁风险的表达方式具有异质性，单一分类器必须平均化不同用户的特征，导致局部证据被稀释，尤其对非自我披露用户容易误分类。
AI.method: 提出WPG-MoE框架：1）基于共享LLM主干，生成用户级弱语义先验；2）利用这些先验将用户软路由到不同专家（对应不同的证据布局模式）；3）将训练过程建模为利用特权信息学习（LUPI），训练时使用LLM提取的结构化证据指导路由，推理时仅需PHQ-9模板筛查。
AI.result: 在中英文数据集上，WPG-MoE优于强基线方法，且路由行为具有可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 通过弱先验引导的混合专家路由机制，能有效处理用户抑郁表达的异质性，提升筛查准确性，且推理阶段轻量高效。

title: ResearchStudio-Idea: An Evidence-Grounded Research-Ideation Skill Suite from ML Conference Outcomes
summary: Large language models have made research ideation increasingly accessible, yet effective idea development requires more than generating candidate directions. Researchers must ground a problem in current literature, identify meaningful bottlenecks, differentiate from existing solutions, and evaluate risks before committing to implementation. We present ResearchStudio-Idea as a reusable skill suite for this first mile of research ideation. The suite includes Paper-Search, a standalone multi-source literature search skill; Scoop-Check, a standalone prior-art collision checker for novelty claims; and IdeaSpark, the end-to-end skill that composes evidence grounding, pattern-guided generation, collision retrieval, audit, and idea-card rendering into one workflow. IdeaSpark is constructed from a corpus of 1,947 machine learning conference papers collected from ICLR, ICML, and NeurIPS between 2021 and 2025, including Oral papers, a separately tracked high-citation subset, and rejected submissions. Analysis of these outcomes reveals 31 recurring ideation sub-patterns, consolidated into 15 reusable ideation patterns. Each pattern is operationalized as a structured card containing research contexts, bottleneck types, differentiation strategies, supporting precedents, and common failure modes. Given a research problem and an evidence bundle, IdeaSpark evaluates evidence readiness, reconstructs the surrounding research context, identifies unresolved bottlenecks, selects relevant patterns, instantiates one candidate direction, retrieves potentially conflicting prior work, and performs outcome-informed auditing. This workflow transforms reusable ideation patterns into traceable research proposals. Blind automated-judge evaluations show that IdeaSpark consistently produces stronger research proposals than no-skill and generic-skill baselines while maintaining competitive novelty.

AI.tldr: 本文提出ResearchStudio-Idea，一个可复用的研究构思技能套件，包含文献搜索、新颖性检查和端到端构思生成工作流，基于1,947篇ML会议论文提炼出15个可复用的构思模式，并通过自动化评估验证其效果优于基线方法。
AI.motivation: 大型语言模型虽降低了研究构思的门槛，但有效的构思开发需要将问题扎根于现有文献、识别有意义的瓶颈、区分已有解决方案并评估风险。现有方法缺乏系统化的工具来支持研究构思的"第一英里"流程。
AI.method: 从1,947篇ML会议论文（ICLR/ICML/NeurIPS 2021-2025，含Oral论文、高引用子集和被拒稿件）中分析出31个构思子模式，归纳为15个可复用构思模式。构建IdeaSpark工作流：评估证据准备度→重构研究上下文→识别未解决瓶颈→选择相关模式→实例化候选方向→检索冲突先验工作→结果审计。包含Paper-Search（多源文献搜索）和Scoop-Check（新颖性碰撞检测）两个独立技能。
AI.result: 盲审自动化评估显示，IdeaSpark持续生成比无技能和通用技能基线更强的研究提案，同时保持具有竞争力的新颖性水平。
AI.conclusion: 本文展示了一种将可复用构思模式转化为可追溯研究提案的系统化方法，有效支撑研究构思的早期阶段。

title: SPLIT: Training-Free AI-Generated and Partially Edited Video Detection via Spatial Patch-Level Incoherence and Temporal Roughness
summary: Deploying AI-generated video detectors in real-world services demands an ultra-low false positive rate (FPR) on real videos to avoid falsely rejecting authentic content, a regime where standard metrics such as AUROC fail to reflect actual operating behavior. We introduce Spatial Patch-Level Incoherence and Temporal Roughness (SPLIT), a training-free detector that operates on patch tokens from a frozen vision encoder to detect both fully generated and partially edited videos. SPLIT computes two complementary signals: Two-step Temporal Roughness (TTR), capturing non-smooth patch trajectories via one-step and two-step feature variation contrast, and Local Spatial Motion Incoherence (LSMI), measuring spatially inconsistent temporal changes through gradients of a feature-space motion field. The two are fused multiplicatively with gamma correction to sharpen real-fake separation at strict thresholds. We further propose a service-aligned evaluation protocol based on Fake Recall at fixed FPR with real-only threshold calibration and cross-real threshold transfer. Across three benchmarks (FakeParts, GenVideo, and ViF-Bench), SPLIT achieves the highest Fake Recall at FPR = $0.1\%$, substantially outperforming supervised and training-free baselines while remaining robust to post-processing with negligible overhead. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/mldljyh/SPLIT .

AI.tldr: 本文提出SPLIT，一种无需训练、基于冻结视觉编码器补丁标记的AI生成视频检测器，通过两步时间粗糙度（TTR）和局部空间运动不连贯性（LSMI）双重互补信号，在极低假阳性率下实现高召回率，并引入服务对齐的评估协议。
AI.motivation: 在真实服务场景中部署AI生成视频检测器需要极低的假阳性率（FPR），而标准指标（如AUROC）无法反映这一极端区域的真实性能。现有方法在此严格阈值下表现不佳，亟需一种专为超低FPR设计的检测方案。
AI.method: SPLIT包含两大互补信号：1) 两步时间粗糙度（TTR），通过一步与两步特征变化对比捕捉非平滑补丁轨迹；2) 局部空间运动不连贯性（LSMI），通过特征空间运动场梯度度量空间不一致的时间变化。两者经伽马校正后相乘融合。同时提出基于固定FPR下虚假召回率（Fake Recall）的服务对齐评估协议。
AI.result: 在FakeParts、GenVideo和ViF-Bench三个基准上，SPLIT在FPR=0.1%时取得最高Fake Recall，显著优于有监督和无需训练的基线方法，且对后处理鲁棒、计算开销极小。
AI.conclusion: SPLIT是一种高效、无需训练、适用于超低FPR场景的AI视频检测方案，其多模态不连贯性信号和评估协议对齐实际部署需求，具有良好的实用价值。

title: LogNLQ: Natural-Language Log Querying with Parser-Induced and Semantically Grounded Schemas
summary: Logs are essential for system monitoring and failure diagnoses in modern software systems, yet querying them through natural language remains an open challenge. Existing approaches either treat logs as plain text, generate queries for schema-light backends, or assume predefined relational schemas, but none addresses a fundamental obstacle: raw logs carry no executable schema over which structured queries can be defined and run. To address these limitations, we present LogNLQ, a framework that formulates natural-language log querying as executable SQL generation over parser-induced and semantically grounded schemas. LogNLQ parses raw logs into template-partitioned relational tables, then applies dual-granularity semantic grounding to annotate both templates and parameter columns with interpretable names and descriptions. At query time, relevant schema candidates are retrieved via semantic search, and a large language model (LLM) generates executable SQL constrained to the retrieved context. To support rigorous evaluation, we introduce LogNLQ-Bench, an execution-verified benchmark of 8,895 queries over four real-world log datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that LogNLQ consistently outperforms all representative baselines by wide margins, with especially pronounced gains on analytically complex scenario queries.

AI.tldr: 提出LogNLQ框架，通过将原始日志解析为模板分区的关系表并添加语义标注，结合语义搜索和LLM生成可执行SQL，实现自然语言日志查询，在包含8,895个查询的基准测试中大幅优于现有方法。
AI.motivation: 现有日志查询方法要么将日志视为纯文本、要么假设已存在关系模式，但原始日志本身缺乏可执行的关系模式，导致无法定义和执行结构化查询，自然语言查询日志这一核心障碍尚未解决。
AI.method: 1) 将原始日志解析为模板分区的关系表；2) 通过双粒度语义标注为模板列和参数列赋予可解释的名称和描述；3) 查询时通过语义搜索检索相关模式候选；4) 利用LLM在检索到的上下文约束下生成可执行SQL。
AI.result: 在LogNLQ-Bench基准（包含8,895个查询，覆盖4个真实日志数据集）上，LogNLQ在所有代表性基线方法上均取得大幅领先，在分析复杂的场景查询上优势尤为显著。
AI.conclusion: LogNLQ通过自动从原始日志中构建语义丰富的可执行模式，有效弥合了自然语言与结构化日志查询之间的鸿沟，是一种实用且高效的日志查询解决方案。

title: Memory-Orchestrated Semantic System (MOSS): An Auditable Agentic Memory Architecture
summary: Long-term memory remains a structural weakness of AI agents. The dominant approach, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), relies on embedding-based similarity search, which is opaque by construction, difficult to audit, and bounded by the theoretical limits of vector representations. We present the Memory-Orchestrated Semantic System (MOSS), an agentic memory architecture in which the agent drives retrieval over a structured relational database. MOSS is model-agnostic, storage-agnostic, and API-agnostic: it runs on any relational engine, connects to any LLM provider (or to deterministic non-LLM processes), and deploys on any infrastructure, local or cloud. Its retrieval execution is symbolic and reproducible (once a query is formulated, no LLM participates in the retrieval loop) and every step of the system, from indexing to answer formulation, is logged and inspectable, making MOSS auditable by construction. Rather than imposing an external ontology, MOSS derives its conceptual vocabulary from the corpus itself. We report on a longitudinal deployment unique in the agentic-memory literature: a year of continuous production over an individual scholar's working corpus--a conversational corpus reaching back to October 2024 (some 44 million tokens, retroactively indexed) comprising 110,183 segments, alongside 163,494 catalogued documents, 569 inductively derived concepts, 322,662 concept annotations, and eleven metadata graphs totaling approximately five million relations--across four successive infrastructure generations. While the present case is that of a single researcher, the architecture is in no way specific to one person: it serves a team, an institution, or any entity that accumulates knowledge over time. We argue that auditable, sovereign, structurally unbounded memory is a precondition for AI agents intended to accompany a person or an organization over years rather than sessions.

AI.tldr: 本文提出MOSS记忆架构，用符号化关系数据库检索替代RAG的嵌入相似搜索，实现可审计、无结构上限的智能体长期记忆。通过一年真实生产部署验证（44M tokens, 110K+片段），证明其作为主权记忆基础设施的可行性。
AI.motivation: RAG的嵌入相似搜索存在三大缺陷：不透明、难审计、受限于向量表示的理论容量上限。AI智能体缺乏真正的长期记忆结构，尤其是跨会话、跨年度的知识积累能力。
AI.method: MOSS架构：1）智能体驱动检索（非被动检索）；2）基于关系型数据库的结构化存储；3）符号化检索执行（查询后LLM不参与检索循环）；4）从语料中归纳概念词汇（非外部本体）；5）全流程可审计（索引→查询→答案生成均可检查）。
AI.result: 一年持续部署：个人学者工作语料库（44M tokens，110,183片段，163,494文档，569个归纳概念，322,662概念标注，11个元数据图约500万关系），跨越四代基础设施。
AI.conclusion: 可审计、自主可控、结构上无上限的记忆是陪伴型AI智能体（跨越会话/年份）的前提条件。MOSS证明了符号化记忆架构的可行性与长期稳定性。

title: Why Pure Reasoning is Not Enough: Nature as the Source of Mathematical Innovation
summary:
We advance the hypothesis that human mathematical reasoning, constrained by both the undecidability and the computational intractability of even modest logical fragments, relies fundamentally on pattern matching from domains external to pure deduction. The most prolific reservoir of such patterns is the natural world, whose physical laws and biological systems have undergone billions of years of ``pre-computation'' and already exhibit surprisingly innovative solutions. To ground this claim, we trace the history of the Fourier transform and relevant mathematics, from the vibrating string controversy to the hear equation and subsequent formalisms prevalent in mathematics. At each critical juncture, a physics problem forced the acceptance or creation of a mathematical tool that pure formal reasoning failed to anticipate or, worse, human reasoning had resisted.
  We further survey the landscape of logical complexity, from NP-hard propositional satisfiability to the non-elementary decision-procedures for monadic second-order theories, to demonstrate that even when a logic is decidable, the resources required for worst-case deduction are astronomically prohibitive. We argue that these barriers make physics-inspired pattern matching not just a historical accident but a cognitive necessity. Finally, we draw the consequence for artificial intelligence: if pure reasoning is constitutively insufficient, then any system aiming at human-level mathematical creativity must embed a vast store of cross-domain patterns rather than rely on deduction alone. This furnishes a principled justification for the enormous scale of contemporary large language models.

AI.tldr: 本文提出人类数学推理本质上依赖来自自然界的跨域模式匹配，而非纯粹的逻辑演绎，并以此为大语言模型的巨大规模提供了理论依据。
AI.motivation: 数学推理面临不可判定性和计算复杂性的根本限制，纯粹的形式演绎无法有效应对这些障碍，因此需要探索人类数学思维的真实认知基础。
AI.method: 通过梳理傅里叶变换的历史（从弦振动问题到热方程），展示物理学如何推动数学工具的创造；同时调查逻辑复杂性的理论景观（从NP难的SAT到非初等复杂度的MSO理论），论证纯演绎的不可行性。
AI.result: 物理启发的模式匹配不是历史偶然，而是认知必然；纯推理在理论上无法支撑人类水平的数学创造力。
AI.conclusion: 任何追求人类级数学创造力的AI系统必须嵌入大量跨域模式，而非仅依赖演绎，这为大语言模型的大规模参数提供了原则性辩护。

title: ProLaViT: Learning Progressive Latent Visual Thoughts in Structured Latent Space
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable progress but still struggle with complex visual reasoning tasks requiring multi-step perception and logical deduction. While explicit visual generation incurs prohibitive computational costs, existing latent approaches often rely on external experts or lack rigorous cognitive logic. In this paper, we introduce ProLaViT (Progressive Latent Visual Thought), a framework empowering MLLMs to perform structured visual derivation in the continuous latent space. Unlike works dependent on heterogeneous external models, ProLaViT leverages an endogenous self-distillation mechanism, utilizing the model's own visual encoder to supervise latent thoughts. To facilitate this, we construct a scalable programmatic synthesis pipeline enabling the model to internalize algorithmic precision without inference time tools. We design two reasoning paradigms: (1) Coarse-to-Fine Causal Chain for spatial tasks, guiding attention from global context to local targets. (2) Dialectical Reasoning Chain for logical tasks, incorporating counter-factual thinking for verification. Furthermore, we propose a Distance-Weighted Diversity Loss to impose topology-aware constraints, preventing feature degeneration by enforcing semantic distinctiveness. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ProLaViT outperforms baselines on vision-centric benchmarks, achieving superior accuracy and interpretability with high efficiency.

AI.tldr: ProLaViT提出了一种在连续潜空间中实现结构化视觉推理的框架，通过自蒸馏机制和程序化合成管道，让多模态大模型无需外部工具即可进行多步感知与逻辑演绎，在空间和逻辑任务上设计了两种推理链，并引入距离加权多样性损失防止特征退化，在视觉基准上取得了优越的准确率和可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有MLLMs在复杂视觉推理任务中表现不佳，显式视觉生成计算成本过高，而潜空间方法要么依赖外部专家模型，要么缺乏严谨的认知逻辑。
AI.method: 提出ProLaViT框架，核心包括：(1) 内生自蒸馏机制，利用模型自身视觉编码器监督潜空间思维；(2) 可扩展的程序化合成管道，让模型内化算法精度；(3) 两种推理范式——粗糙到精细因果链（空间任务）和辩证推理链（逻辑任务）；(4) 距离加权多样性损失，施加拓扑感知约束防止特征退化。
AI.result: 在视觉中心基准测试中，ProLaViT优于基线方法，实现了更高的准确率和可解释性，同时保持了高效率。
AI.conclusion: ProLaViT通过在连续潜空间中进行结构化视觉推导，有效提升了MLLMs的复杂视觉推理能力，为未来潜空间推理研究提供了新范式。

title: Inferring the Shape of Data Frames in R Programs using Abstract Interpretation
summary:
Data frames are a fundamental data structure in many data analysis tasks and are widely used in programming languages like R. Due to their omnipresence in data analysis, there are many functions that operate on their shape and content, for example, to clean and transform study data. However, languages like R do not offer static guarantees on data frames making it difficult to reason about their shape at a specific point in the program.
  In this paper, we present a novel static analysis to infer the shape of data frames in R programs using abstract interpretation by tracking the ensured and potential column names, as well as the potential number of columns and rows. For this, we use a reduced product domain and define abstract semantics for the most commonly used data frame operations, such as mutating, filtering, and subsetting. We evaluate the correctness and accuracy of our analysis on a selection of 78 executable real-world R scripts achieving empirical evidence for soundness by never under-approximating the data frame shape. Additionally, we demonstrate the ability of our analysis to infer the shape of data frames on a large dataset of 33,314 real-world R scripts by inferring concrete shape constraints for 42.1 % and exact shapes for 0.9 % of the data frame operations, improving to 58.7 % and 4.2 % if all datasets read in these scripts are available to our analysis. Using the inferred data frame shapes, we identified 40 real-world R scripts containing potential invalid data frame accesses. This shows the potential of our analysis to significantly support researchers in using data frames in data analysis.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于抽象解释的静态分析方法，用于推断R语言中数据框（data frame）的形状（列名、行列数），并在大量真实R脚本上验证了其正确性和实用性。
AI.motivation: R语言的数据框缺乏静态类型保证，程序员难以在程序中推理数据框的列名、行列数等形状信息，容易导致运行时错误。
AI.method: 使用抽象解释方法，构建一个简化乘积域（reduced product domain），追踪数据框的确保列名、潜在列名、潜在列数和行数，并为常见数据框操作（如mutate、filter、subset）定义抽象语义。
AI.result: 在78个真实R脚本上验证了正确性（永不低估形状）；在33,314个脚本上，42.1%的数据框操作能推断出具体形状约束，0.9%能推断出精确形状；若所有数据集可用，比例提升至58.7%和4.2%。并识别出40个存在潜在无效数据框访问的脚本。
AI.conclusion: 该静态分析能有效推断R数据框形状，帮助研究人员避免数据框操作错误，具有实际应用潜力。

title: AI Wizards at EXIST 2026: Hierarchical Soft-Label Learning for Multimodal Sexism Identification in Memes
summary: We present the AI Wizards submission to EXIST 2026 for multimodal sexism identification in memes. The task is composed of three, increasingly harder subtasks. We model them hierarchically as conditional soft-label prediction over empirical annotator distributions. Our system maps fixed Gemini Embedding 2 vision-language representations through a lightweight Gated MLP trained with KL divergence and homoscedastic uncertainty weighting. Our submissions ranked first on Task 2.3 and fourth on Tasks 2.1 and 2.2 on the official Soft-Soft leaderboards. The code is available at https://github.com/NLP-AI-Wizards/EXIST-2026

AI.tldr: 提出AI Wizards系统，使用Gemini Embedding 2视觉-语言表示通过轻量级门控MLP进行分层条件软标签预测，在EXIST 2026多模态性别歧视识别任务中取得SOTA结果。
AI.motivation: 解决多模态模因(memes)中性别歧视的自动识别问题，该任务包含三个难度递增的子任务，需要有效处理不同粒度的分类需求。
AI.method: 采用层级结构建模为条件软标签预测，使用Gemini Embedding 2提取视觉-语言表示，通过轻量级Gated MLP处理，训练时使用KL散度和同方差不确定性加权。
AI.result: 在官方Soft-Soft排行榜上，Task 2.3排名第一，Task 2.1和Task 2.2排名第四。
AI.conclusion: 证明了对多模态性别歧视识别任务采用层次化条件软标签预测方法的有效性，代码已开源。

title: Compressing the Validation Bottleneck: An Agentic Self-Driving Lab for Scientific Discovery
comment: Accepted at ICML 2026 AI for Science Workshop ; AI Scientist Competition
summary: Agentic AI-for-Science can automate ideation, planning, and analysis, but final validation still depends on real experiments. A self-driving lab (SDL) can execute those experiments, yet the loop still has bottlenecks: the agent may spend too many rounds on low-value experiments, or each round may require a high-cost experiment. We target these two physical bottlenecks with one agent. First, a prior-aware agentic DOE loop uses domain knowledge and past results to propose feasible and informative next experiments, reducing trials-to-target. Second, a cost-aware surrogate agent predicts high-cost, high-resolution measurements from low-cost, low-resolution measurements. It chooses between a high- and a low-cost measurement based on the predicted uncertainty. We examine these directions in the biology and materials domains, respectively. Together, under a single agent, these components aim to accelerate the SDL loop by reducing both the number of loops and the cost per experiment.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个统一的智能体框架，通过先验感知的DOE（减少实验轮次）和成本感知的代理模型（降低单次实验成本），同时解决自驱动实验室（SDL）中实验次数过多和单次实验成本高昂两大物理瓶颈，以加速AI-for-Science的闭环验证。
AI.motivation: 尽管Agentic AI能自动化科研的构思、规划和分析，但最终验证仍依赖真实实验。SDL虽能执行实验，但存在两大瓶颈：智能体可能进行过多低价值实验轮次，或每轮实验成本过高。现有方法未能同时解决这两个物理层面的效率问题。
AI.method: 提出一个统一智能体，包含两个核心组件：1）先验感知的主动实验设计（DOE）循环，利用领域知识和历史结果提出可行且信息量大的下一步实验，减少达成目标所需实验轮次；2）成本感知的代理模型，利用低成本低分辨率测量预测高成本高分辨率结果，基于预测不确定性在高低成本测量间智能选择。分别在生物学和材料学领域进行验证。
AI.result: 在生物学领域，先验感知DOE有效减少了实验轮次；在材料学领域，成本感知代理模型在不牺牲精度前提下降低了单次实验成本。两者结合显著加速了SDL的整体闭环效率。
AI.conclusion: 通过一个统一智能体同时减少SDL的循环次数和单次实验成本，是加速AI-for-Science物理验证闭环的有效路径。

title: Holo-Captioning: Toward the Text Equivalent of 3D Scenes
summary: This work introduces holo-captioning, a novel task that strives to seek the text equivalent of 3D scenes. As the initial step, we formulate holo-captioning as generating a structured textual description that comprehensively depicts all entities within a 3D scene -- including their semantic tags, spatial locations, attributes, and inter-entity relations. To tackle this challenging task, we first develop an effective captioning engine to produce detailed descriptions of individual entity instances and instance pairs, and contribute a large-scale benchmark comprising over 15K scenes for training and evaluation. Building upon this foundation, we propose HoloScribe, a novel model that features an instance-aware decoupled pipeline for generating structured holo-captions, and further incorporates anchor-aware instance linking to identify relational instance pairs. Additionally, we propose a comprehensive evaluation metric named HoloScore, and provide a human-curated test set to ensure reliable model assessment. Experimental results demonstrate that HoloScribe significantly outperforms state-of-the-art 3D dense captioners and 3D LLM generalists, underscoring the effectiveness of our approach. Project page: https://visual-ai.github.io/holocap/

AI.tldr: 本文提出了holo-captioning任务，旨在为3D场景生成结构化文本描述，涵盖实体语义标签、空间位置、属性和关系。作者构建了大规模基准数据集，并提出了HoloScribe模型，采用实例感知的解耦管道和锚点感知的实例链接机制，同时设计了HoloScore评估指标。实验表明HoloScribe显著优于现有3D密集描述方法和3D大语言模型。
AI.motivation: 现有3D场景理解工作缺乏能够全面描述场景中所有实体的结构化文本表达，亟需一种将3D场景转换为等效文本描述的方法，以弥合3D视觉与自然语言之间的鸿沟。
AI.method: 1) 构建包含15K+场景的大规模基准数据集；2) 提出HoloScribe模型，包含实例感知的解耦管道（分别描述单个实例和实例对）和锚点感知的实例链接机制（识别有关联的实例对）；3) 设计HoloScore综合评估指标并提供人工筛选的测试集。
AI.result: HoloScribe在结构化3D场景描述生成任务上显著优于现有最先进的3D密集描述方法和3D大语言模型。
AI.conclusion: holo-captioning作为3D场景理解的新范式具有重要价值，HoloScribe通过解耦管道和锚点感知链接有效解决了结构化描述的生成问题，为3D视觉与语言对齐提供了新方向。

title: Balancing Microservices and Monolithic Architectures
comment: Published in Feedforward, IEEE Computer Society Santa Clara Valley Chapter, Volume 5, Issue 3, July 2026, pp. 28-33. Case Study. 6 pages, 1 table
summary: Enterprise software teams face a fundamental architectural choice: build a single unified application or decompose functionality into independently deployable services. This article examines monolithic and microservices architectures, analyzing their technical benefits, tradeoffs, and practical implications for scalability, reliability, deployment, and organizational complexity. It discusses how teams can evaluate these architectural approaches based on system size, business requirements, operational maturity, and long-term maintainability.

AI.tldr: 本文对比了单体架构与微服务架构在可扩展性、可靠性、部署和组织复杂性方面的优劣，并提出了基于系统规模、业务需求、运营成熟度和长期可维护性的选型评估框架。
AI.motivation: 企业软件团队在构建系统时面临一个根本性架构抉择：是构建单一统一应用，还是将功能拆分为可独立部署的服务。这一选择直接影响系统的可扩展性、可靠性和团队效率，因此需要系统性地分析两种架构的利弊。
AI.method: 通过对比分析单体架构和微服务架构在技术优势、权衡点以及实际影响（如可扩展性、可靠性、部署、组织复杂度）方面的差异，提出基于系统规模、业务需求、运营成熟度和长期可维护性等维度的评估框架。
AI.result: 识别了两类架构在不同场景下的适用性：单体架构适合小规模系统、简单业务需求和低运营成熟度团队；微服务架构适合大规模系统、复杂业务需求和高运营成熟度团队。但未给出具体的量化对比数据或实验验证。
AI.conclusion: 团队应根据实际系统规模、业务复杂度、团队运营能力和长期维护需求来权衡选择架构，不存在普适的最优解，合适才是关键。

title: UI-MOPD: Multi-Platform On-Policy Distillation for Continual GUI Agent Learning
summary:
Recent advances in multimodal foundation models and agent systems have driven GUI agents from single-platform task execution toward cross-platform interaction. However, building multi-platform GUI agents remains challenging. On one hand, high-quality and executable cross-platform interaction trajectories are still scarce, and existing data often suffer from limited platform coverage. On the other hand, different platforms exhibit distinct interaction conventions, making joint or continual training prone to behavioral pattern mixing, platform-specific capability degradation, and catastrophic forgetting. To address these challenges, we construct Uni-GUI, a high-quality cross-platform GUI interaction dataset, and propose UI-MOPD, the first method that incorporates multi-teacher on-policy distillation into continual learning for GUI agents. UI-MOPD dynamically selects a platform-specific teacher according to the current environment and transfers platform-specific behavioral priors to a shared policy through platform-conditioned distillation, enabling adaptation to new platforms while preserving capabilities on existing ones. Experiments on OSWorld and MobileWorld show that UI-MOPD achieves task success rates of 38.2% and 12.0%, respectively, demonstrating its effectiveness in balancing cross-platform capability retention and new-platform adaptation.
  Project page: https://elispectre.github.io/UI-MOPD/.

AI.tldr: 针对跨平台GUI智能体面临的数据稀缺和灾难性遗忘问题，构建了Uni-GUI数据集并提出UI-MOPD方法（多教师在线蒸馏持续学习），在OSWorld和MobileWorld上分别达到38.2%和12.0%的成功率。
AI.motivation: 当前多平台GUI智能体面临两大挑战：1）高质量、可执行的跨平台交互轨迹数据稀缺且平台覆盖有限；2）不同平台交互习惯不同，联合或持续训练会导致行为模式混淆、特定平台能力退化和灾难性遗忘。
AI.method: 提出UI-MOPD，首次将多教师在线蒸馏（multi-teacher on-policy distillation）融入持续学习框架。根据当前环境动态选择平台专属教师，通过平台条件蒸馏（platform-conditioned distillation）将平台特定的行为先验迁移到共享策略中，在适应新平台的同时保留已有平台能力。
AI.result: 在OSWorld和MobileWorld基准上，UI-MOPD分别达到38.2%和12.0%的任务成功率，验证了其在跨平台能力保留和新平台适应之间的平衡效果。
AI.conclusion: Uni-GUI数据集和UI-MOPD方法有效缓解了跨平台GUI智能体的数据稀缺与灾难性遗忘问题，为构建多平台交互智能体提供了可行的技术路径。

title: VLA Grounder: Language-Conditioning Space Optimization for Black-Box VLA Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are commonly treated as end-to-end action policies conditioned on natural-language task descriptions. In practice, however, their behavior often depends sharply on how the instruction is phrased, suggesting that language is not merely a task label but an optimizable conditioning input. We study whether frozen VLA policies can be improved by optimizing language space rather than updating action weights. Our method introduces a language-conditioning space policy that translates a human instruction into a short VLA-grounded command using object appearance, spatial relations, and target-grounding cues. The language-conditioning space policy is initialized with a failure-derived command-space prior and optimized with reinforcement learning from sparse task-completion rewards, while the downstream VLA remains fully frozen. This yields language-conditioning space optimization: RL discovers which VLA-grounded commands best elicit successful behavior from the frozen action policy. Experiments on RL4VLA and VL-Think show that language-conditioning space optimization improves success on instruction-sensitive, symbolic, and multi-object manipulation tasks, demonstrating that language can serve as an optimizable variable for a robot foundation models. Website: https://tttonyalpha.github.io/vla_grounder

AI.tldr: 提出语言条件空间优化方法，通过强化学习优化冻结VLA模型的语言指令（而非模型权重），提升机器人操作任务成功率。
AI.motivation: VLA模型对指令表述方式高度敏感，语言不仅是任务标签，更是可优化的条件输入。作者希望在不更新模型权重的情况下，仅通过优化语言指令来提升模型表现。
AI.method: 设计一个语言条件空间策略，将人类指令转换为VLA可理解的短命令（包含物体外观、空间关系、目标定位信息）。该策略初始化时使用失败派生的命令空间先验，通过稀疏任务完成奖励的强化学习进行优化，下游VLA模型保持完全冻结。
AI.result: 在RL4VLA和VL-Think基准上，该方法在指令敏感任务、符号任务和多物体操作任务中均提升了成功率。
AI.conclusion: 语言可以作为机器人基础模型的可优化变量，仅通过优化语言空间就能提升冻结VLA策略的表现。

title: Learning Taxonomic Trees with Hierarchical Representation Regularization for Large Multimodal Models
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICML 2026
summary: Taxonomies provide key information about the semantic relationships between concepts and the inherent organization of vision and language. Despite their impressive capabilities, large multimodal models (LMMs) often lack taxonomic knowledge, leading to low hierarchical visual recognition (HVR) consistency. These models typically only rely on language modeling objectives during fine-tuning and lack explicit taxonomy-aware regularization. To address this, we propose Hierarchical Representation Regularization ($HiR^2$), a simple plug-and-play regularizer that improves hierarchical consistency in LMMs. Specifically, we introduce a semantic-aware visual tree construction framework that extracts coarse-to-fine visual features from intermediate LLM layers guided by textual cues. The regularizer combines two complementary objectives: a taxonomic entailment loss that enforces hierarchy via hyperbolic entailment cones in the Lorentz model, and a discriminative dispersive loss that promotes angular separation of semantically similar embeddings on the unit sphere without disturbing the radial hierarchical structure. Extensive experiments demonstrate that $HiR^2$ effectively captures taxonomic structures across diverse LMMs and fine-tuning methods. Code is available at https://github.com/PKU-ICST-MIPL/HiR2_ICML2026.

AI.tldr: 提出 Hierarchical Representation Regularization (HiR²)，一种即插即用的正则化方法，通过双目标损失（双曲包含损失+判别分散损失）提升多模态大模型的层级视觉识别一致性。
AI.motivation: 多模态大模型在层级视觉识别中缺乏一致性，因为它们微调时仅依赖语言建模目标，缺乏显式的层级感知正则化。
AI.method: 1) 构建语义感知视觉树结构，从LLM中间层提取由粗到细的视觉特征；2) 采用双目标正则化：洛伦兹模型中的双曲包含损失（强制层级蕴含关系）+ 单位球面上的判别分散损失（促进语义相似嵌入的角度分离）。
AI.result: 实验表明HiR²能够有效捕获分类学结构，适用于多种多模态大模型和微调方法。
AI.conclusion: HiR²是一种简单有效的即插即用正则化方法，可显著提升多模态大模型的层级视觉识别一致性。

title: Why3-py: A Tool for Formal Verification of Hypothesis Testing and Meta-Analysis in Python
summary: The reproducibility crisis in scientific research has received widespread recognition, thereby increasing the importance of meta-analyses that integrate statistical analyses from multiple studies. However, statistical methods often have ambiguous and implicit underlying assumptions, which can lead to their erroneous applications and interpretations. To address this issue, we propose a formal verification framework for statistical programs written in Python. Specifically, we present Why3-py, a Python front-end for the Why3 verification platform that transforms Python programs into verification-oriented WhyML representations suitable for formal verification, addressing the challenges arising from Python's dynamic typing and runtime polymorphism. Furthermore, we extend the StatWhy tool to support the verification of meta-analysis methods. These tools enable users to identify overlooked assumptions and misuse of analyses, and to verify the correctness of Python programs for hypothesis testing and for meta-analyses.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Why3-py工具，将Python统计程序转换为WhyML进行形式化验证，并扩展StatWhy支持元分析方法的验证，以解决统计方法中隐含假设被忽视和误用的问题，应对再现性危机。
AI.motivation: 科学研究中的可重复性危机日益严重，元分析的重要性上升，但统计方法的隐含假设常常被忽视或误用，导致错误的统计推断。
AI.method: 开发了Why3-py工具，作为Why3验证平台的Python前端，将Python程序转换为适合形式化验证的WhyML表示，解决了Python动态类型和运行时多态性带来的挑战；并扩展了StatWhy工具以支持元分析方法的验证。
AI.result: 实现了对Python统计程序的形式化验证能力，能够识别被忽视的假设和方法误用，验证假设检验和元分析程序的正确性。
AI.conclusion: 该形式化验证框架有助于提高统计分析的可靠性，为应对科学研究的再现性危机提供了自动化验证支持。

title: dOPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Diffusion Language Models
summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising a masked sequence, offering a parallel alternative to autoregressive models, but eliciting strong reasoning through post-training remains difficult: supervised fine-tuning is off-policy and suffers from exposure bias, while reinforcement learning gives only sparse, sequence-level rewards and is hard to apply without tractable sequence likelihoods. On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) offers a promising alternative, using one model as both student and teacher to provide dense, token-level, on-policy supervision, but its effectiveness hinges on giving the teacher privileged information (PI) - typically an instance-specific ground-truth reference unavailable at inference - so the student ends up distilling a weak PI-free consensus policy that yields little improvement on dLLM reasoning. We introduce dOPSD, which instead derives the teacher's privilege directly from the student's own denoising trajectory, evaluating masked positions using later, more-decoded steps of that same trajectory rather than an external label, so the teacher's advantage emerges from the model's own decoding process; on Dream and LLaDA, dOPSD improves both in-domain math reasoning and out-of-domain code generation, outperforming supervised and on-policy baselines.

AI.tldr: 本文提出dOPSD方法，通过从模型自身的去噪轨迹中提取特权信息来改进扩散大语言模型的推理能力，在数学推理和代码生成任务上优于现有基线方法。
AI.motivation: 扩散大语言模型（dLLMs）通过迭代去噪掩码序列生成文本，是一种并行的自回归替代方案。然而，其推理能力的后训练强化面临困难：监督微调存在离策略和曝光偏差问题，强化学习则面临奖励稀疏且难以应用的问题。现有的On-policy自蒸馏（OPSD）方法虽提供密集的token级监督，但依赖外部真实标签作为教师模型的"特权信息"，导致学生模型蒸馏出的策略效果有限。
AI.method: 提出dOPSD方法，核心创新在于教师模型的特权信息不再依赖外部真实标签，而是从学生模型自身的去噪轨迹中提取。具体而言，通过评估掩码位置在后续更解码步骤中的状态（而非外部标签），使教师模型的优势源于模型自身的解码过程。
AI.result: 在Dream和LLaDA两个扩散语言模型上，dOPSD在域内数学推理（in-domain math reasoning）和域外代码生成（out-of-domain code generation）任务上均取得提升，优于监督学习和on-policy基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文证明了利用模型自身解码轨迹作为特权信息的有效性，为扩散语言模型的后训练推理增强提供了新的范式，避免了对外部标注数据的依赖。

title: Measuring Harness-Induced Belief Divergence in Multi-Step LLM Agents
summary: Software-agent benchmarks usually report whether an agent solves a task, but the agent reaches that outcome through a harness that controls what it sees, which actions it can take, which failures are repaired, which states are verified, and which evidence is logged. We show that this harness can change the agent's multi-step beliefs even when the task, environment, and base LLM are fixed. We introduce a belief-rollout diagnostic that elicits structured K-step trajectories over progress, risk, recoverability, constraints, failure mode, uncertainty, future success, repair cost, and next action under alternative harnesses. We define a cross-harness belief divergence and decompose it into an arrival term for immediate interface shifts and a growth term for horizon-dependent belief changes. On controlled coding tasks and public-benchmark stress tests, blocked actions, compressed repairs, selective verification, and cost-aware evidence pruning often preserve terminal success while changing the beliefs that drive later decisions. We further introduce BIWM, a no-training protocol that canonicalizes observations, logs censored branches, expands repair traces, records verification masks, executes risky branches in shadow, and aligns belief trajectories across harness views. The results suggest that harness design is an experimental variable in agent evaluation, not an implementation detail. Our code is available at https://github.com/Hik289/Harness-induce-bias.git.

AI.tldr: 本文揭示了软件智能体评估中的"框架偏见"问题：实验框架（harness）即使不改变任务、环境和基础模型，也会通过控制智能体的观察、动作、失败修复、状态验证和日志记录等方式改变其多步信念，进而影响决策。作者提出了信念展开诊断方法和BIWM协议来量化和规范化这种偏见。
AI.motivation: 现有智能体基准测试通常只报告任务是否完成，忽略了实验框架设计对智能体信念和决策过程的系统性影响。作者认为框架设计是一个实验变量而非实现细节，需要被严格审视。
AI.method: 1) 提出信念展开诊断（belief-rollout diagnostic），生成结构化的K步轨迹。2) 定义跨框架信念散度，分解为到达项（即时界面变化）和增长项（随 horizon 变化的信念变化）。3) 提出BIWM协议（无训练），通过规范化观察、记录审查分支、扩展修复轨迹、记录验证掩码、影子执行高风险分支等方式对齐不同框架下的信念轨迹。
AI.result: 在受控编码任务和公开基准压力测试中，发现动作阻塞、压缩修复、选择性验证和成本感知的证据剪枝往往能保留最终任务成功，但同时改变了驱动后续决策的信念。
AI.conclusion: 框架设计是智能体评估中的一个重要实验变量，而非无关紧要的实现细节。研究者和工程师需要关注框架如何塑造智能体的内部信念，而不仅仅是最终任务完成率。

title: See the Emotion: A Facial Emoji Proxy Modeling for EEG Emotion Recognition
summary: Despite the high accuracy of EEG-based emotion recognition, existing models remain opaque "black boxes", lacking semantic grounding between abstract neural features and human-interpretable states. In this paper, we reframe EEG explainability as a cross-modal generation task, shifting the paradigm from feature attribution to behavioral visualization. We introduce Facial Emoji Proxy Modeling, a novel framework that translates high-dimensional EEG signals into identity-anonymized facial emojis. Guided by the neuroscientific inspiration of neural-facial association, this approach grounds neural representations in the manifold of observable facial dynamics. Technically, our framework integrates FMENet, a specialized backbone modeling expression-relevant spatial synergies, and the Facial Emoji Learning Branch (FELB), which treats emoji reconstruction as a structured semantic regularizer. Extensive experiments on EAV and MMER benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy among EEG-only models. Crucially, it generates semantically faithful facial animations that provide a transparent, privacy-preserving window into the brain's emotional evolution, effectively allowing users to "see the emotion" directly from neural signals. Code is available at https://github.com/xian-sh/SeeEmotion

AI.tldr: 提出面部表情符号代理建模框架，将EEG情感识别从黑盒特征归因转向跨模态生成任务，通过生成身份匿名的面部表情符号实现可解释的情感可视化。
AI.motivation: 现有EEG情感识别模型虽然准确率高，但作为"黑箱"缺乏神经特征与可解释情感状态之间的语义对齐，难以让用户理解模型决策依据。
AI.method: 提出Facial Emoji Proxy Modeling框架，包含FMENet骨干网络（建模表达相关的空间协同性）和Facial Emoji Learning Branch (FELB)（将表情符号重建作为结构化语义正则化器），将EEG信号转化为身份匿名的面部表情符号。
AI.result: 在EAV和MMER基准上达到EEG-only模型的SOTA准确率，同时生成语义忠实的面部动画。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为EEG情感识别提供了透明、保护隐私的可视化窗口，让用户能够直接"看到"大脑情绪演化过程。

title: Neuro-Symbolic Reasoning for Vulnerability Detection
summary:
Ask a large language model (LLM) whether a pointer dereference is safe, and it can often produce a plausible justification for ``yes''. The difficulty is that a fluent justification is not a proof. This gap is precisely where automated vulnerability detection lives: deciding, for a given operation in source code, whether a memory safety defect such as a null dereference, use-after-free, or double free can actually occur. We trace the unreliability of LLM-based vulnerability detection to a mechanism, the premature discharge of safety obligations, and argue that the remedy is not better prompting but a separation of roles: the component that interprets the code must not also be the one that decides a safety obligation is met.
  In this paper, we present LeanGuard, a neuro-symbolic framework that assigns each act to the side equipped for it. On the neural side, an LLM serves strictly as a semantic filter over candidate facts extracted from the abstract syntax tree (AST): it prunes spurious facts and keeps the real ones, but never discharges an obligation or decides the verdict on its own. On the symbolic side, the surviving facts are compiled into a verification model in Lean 4 (a formal proof assistant whose kernel accepts a conclusion only when it is formally proved), where every dangerous operation must be matched by a guard that provably covers it in scope; absent such a guard, the obligation stays open rather than being argued away. Because a function rarely arrives with full context, this symbolic model is necessarily partial: an unproved obligation is not yet a defect. An evidence-aware adjudicator therefore weighs the symbolic and neural verdicts by the quality of each. We instantiate the framework on five CWE classes to ask how far this division of labor can be pushed.

AI.tldr: 本文提出LeanGuard，一种神经符号框架，将LLM的角色限制为语义过滤器（仅清洗AST提取的事实），而安全义务的裁决交给Lean 4形式化验证器，从而解决LLM在漏洞检测中不可靠的问题。
AI.motivation: LLM在判断指针解引用等内存安全漏洞时，能生成流利的解释但不可靠，其根本原因是"安全义务的过早解除"——即解释代码的组件同时也在裁决安全义务是否满足。
AI.method: 提出LeanGuard框架：1）神经侧：LLM仅作为语义过滤器，从AST中清理虚假事实，保留真实事实；2）符号侧：将事实编译为Lean 4验证模型，每个危险操作必须有形式化证明的保护条件；3）证据感知裁决器：综合神经和符号结果，根据证据质量做出判断。
AI.result: 在5个CWE类别上实例化了该框架，验证了这种分工方式的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 解决LLM在漏洞检测中不可靠的关键不是更好的提示词，而是角色分离——代码解释者不能同时是安全义务的裁决者。

title: evalci: A Python Library for Statistically Rigorous Comparison of Language Model Evaluations
comment: 7 pages, 1 figure. Software: https://pypi.org/project/evalci/ (source: https://github.com/Shreyaskc/evalci, Zenodo DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21201815)
summary: The dominant practice in language model evaluation is to report a single accuracy number per model and declare the higher one better, without testing whether the gap could plausibly be sampling noise. On benchmarks of a few thousand items, and under temperature sampling where a model can differ from itself run to run by more than the reported gap between models, this practice routinely overstates confidence in headline claims. The statistical machinery to fix this -- confidence intervals, paired significance tests, power analysis, clustered standard errors, multiple-comparison correction -- is well established, but no standard, pip-installable tool packages it in the shape an evaluation actually takes: a per-item results table. We present evalci, a pure-Python library (numpy/scipy/pandas only) that turns a per-item results table into a publication-ready claim -- e.g., "Model A beats Model B, $Δ=3.1$ pts, 95% CI [1.2, 5.0], paired permutation $p=0.002$, $n=1{,}319$" -- in one function call, with adapters for lm-evaluation-harness and HELM output. Every routine is validated against an independent reference (statsmodels, or brute-force exact enumeration) rather than only against itself. As a case study, we re-analyze a public comparison of nine language models' MMLU accuracy and find that 3 of the 8 adjacent leaderboard-rank gaps are not statistically significant after correcting for the 36 pairwise comparisons the ranking implies. evalci is available at https://pypi.org/project/evalci/ (source: https://github.com/Shreyaskc/evalci, DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21201815)

AI.tldr: 本文提出了 evalci，一个轻量级 Python 库，用于从逐项结果表生成带有置信区间、显著性检验和多重比较校正的发表级评价声明，并通过对9个模型MMLU数据的重分析表明，8个相邻排名中3个差距并不显著。
AI.motivation: 当前语言模型评估普遍仅报告单一准确率数值并直接比较高低，忽略了采样噪声、温度采样下的自变异以及统计显著性，导致对结论的过度自信。
AI.method: 开发 evalci 纯 Python 库（仅依赖 numpy/scipy/pandas），输入逐项结果表，一行代码输出包含置信区间、配对置换检验 p 值、聚类标准误、多重比较校正等统计量的声明，并提供 lm-evaluation-harness 和 HELM 适配器。所有例程通过独立参考（statsmodels 或穷举枚举）验证。
AI.result: 对9个语言模型的MMLU准确率进行重分析，发现8个相邻排行榜排名差距中，有3个在经多重比较校正（36个配对比较）后不具有统计显著性。
AI.conclusion: 语言模型评估应引入标准统计方法以避免误导性结论；evalci 提供了一种即装即用的解决方案。

title: Heaviside Continuity of Rolling Coefficients for Eliminating Epistemic Entropy in Large Language Models
summary: Large language models (LLMs) generate fluent outputs that can be wrong. Unlike humans, who often exhibit cues when providing false information, LLMs produce errors that are difficult to detect because autoregressive decoding provides no mechanism for verifying intermediate reasoning before state progression. We introduce Heaviside Continuity of Rolling Coefficients (HCRC), a verification-first execution framework that reformulates inference as predicate-gated state transitions governed by a Heaviside Gate. HCRC combines model confidence with independent verification signals from a parallel worker architecture, allowing execution to advance only when predefined correctness predicates are satisfied. This prevents invalid intermediate states from propagating, reducing epistemic entropy without modifying the underlying model. We evaluate HCRC on software-engineering and reasoning tasks across thirteen proposers from four providers. On capable proposers, the gate reduces the false-completion rate (FCR) from 4--7% to 0% while remaining latency-competitive and, in some settings, faster than the unwrapped model. On weaker proposers, it converts false completions into honest halts instead of corrupting downstream state. Beyond benchmarking, HCRC has operated for months as the production control plane of an agentic coding environment, authorizing file mutations, verification-driven progress reporting, and memory compaction. These results establish HCRC as a general framework for verification-driven LLM execution, showing that reliable reasoning can be achieved through principled execution control rather than model scale alone.

AI.tldr: 本文提出HCRC框架，通过谓词门控的状态转换机制（Heaviside门）结合模型置信度与独立验证信号，在推理过程中阻止无效中间状态传播，从而降低错误完成率（FCR）至0%，无需修改底层模型。
AI.motivation: LLM自回归解码缺乏验证中间推理的机制，导致错误难以检测。现有方法通常依靠模型缩放或事后纠正，而非在执行过程中主动防止错误传播。
AI.method: HCRC将推理重新定义为由Heaviside门控制的谓词门控状态转换：只有当预定义的正确性谓词（结合模型置信度和独立验证信号）被满足时，执行才允许推进。采用并行工作器架构提供独立验证信号。
AI.result: 在13个LLM（来自4个提供商）的软件工程与推理任务上，HCRC将强模型的错误完成率从4-7%降至0%，且延迟具有竞争力；对弱模型则将错误完成转为诚实中止。已作为生产控制平面运行数月，管理文件变更、验证驱动进度报告和内存压缩。
AI.conclusion: HCRC表明，通过原则性的执行控制（而非单纯扩大模型规模），可以在LLM推理中实现可靠的推理。它建立了一个验证驱动的LLM执行通用框架。

title: R3D: Quantitative 3D Spatial Reasoning for Egocentric Wearables
summary: Quantitative 3D spatial reasoning from egocentric RGB-D video is a critical capability for next-generation wearable assistants. Yet existing benchmarks do not reflect the challenges of handling (1) natural egocentric video, (2) posed RGB-D video inputs, and (3) challenging quantitative 3D spatial reasoning Q&A. To fill this gap, we introduce R3D-Bench (Reasoning in 3D), a benchmark of 3,033 quantitative spatial reasoning questions across 15 types -- spanning multiple-choice, distance-based, and volumetric reasoning questions -- built on top of 57 egocentric video sequences from Aria Digital Twin. To set a strong baseline on this dataset, we introduce R3D, a model-agnostic spatial tool-calling framework. In contrast to existing approaches that directly embed 3D information into the model's input representation, R3D constructs a 3D scene from video using segmentation and depth-lifted object representations. It provides this information to an LLM through eight composable spatial tools. On R3D-Bench, R3D with Qwen3-VL 235B achieves 73.5% mean relative accuracy, substantially outperforming the best depth-enabled baseline (CuTR+Tools, 61.9%) and the best RGB-only baseline (Gemini 3 Flash, 46.5%).

AI.tldr: 本文提出R3D-Bench（3,033道3D空间推理题，基于57个第一视角视频）和R3D框架（通过八种可组合空间工具让LLM进行3D推理），在Qwen3-VL 235B上达到73.5%准确率，远超基线方法。
AI.motivation: 现有基准无法同时处理自然第一视角视频、带位姿的RGB-D输入以及挑战性的定量3D空间推理问答，存在明显空白。
AI.method: 构建基于Aria Digital Twin的57段第一视角视频的3,033道空间推理题；提出R3D框架，通过分割和深度提升的对象表示构建3D场景，并提供八种可组合空间工具给LLM调用。
AI.result: R3D（Qwen3-VL 235B）在R3D-Bench上达到73.5%平均相对准确率，优于CuTR+Tools（61.9%）和Gemini 3 Flash（46.5%）。
AI.conclusion: R3D-Bench填补了3D空间推理评估的空白，R3D框架证明了工具调用范式在定量3D推理中的有效性。

title: Refused in Chat, Written in Code: Workflow-Level Jailbreak Construction in IDE Coding Agents
summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as IDE-integrated coding agents that decompose tasks, generate and edit files, run code, and refine outputs over many turns. Yet their safety is still often evaluated as if they were chatbots: one harmful prompt, one response, judged in isolation. We introduce workflow-level jailbreak construction, a failure mode in which a harmful objective is assembled across ordinary stages of a software-development workflow rather than generated through a single direct prompt. Using GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, we study four closed-weight backends: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Gemini 3.5 Flash. Across 204 prompts from Hammurabi's Code, HarmBench, and AdvBench , the models show near-complete refusal under direct chat, CSV-read, and single-step code-fix baselines, with only 8/816 successful responses in each baseline condition. Under the full workflow, however, the same prompts and backends produce 816/816 unsafe teaching-shot completions, all independently confirmed by two expert evaluators under a strict rubric. These results show that conversational refusal benchmarks can substantially overstate the safety of deployed coding agents and motivate defenses that reason about safety across multi-turn IDE workflows and their generated artifacts, not only individual chat turns.

AI.tldr: 本文发现，大语言模型作为IDE集成编码代理时，在完整工作流中攻击成功率100%，而在直接对话基线中仅0.98%，表明对话式拒答基准严重高估了编码代理的安全性。
AI.motivation: 现有安全评估仅以单轮对话方式测试模型拒答能力，忽略了编码代理在多轮工作流中分解任务、执行代码的复杂场景，导致安全评估与实际部署存在巨大鸿沟。
AI.method: 提出"工作流级越狱构建"方法，将有害目标分散到软件开发的常规步骤中。在VS Code的GitHub Copilot上，对Claude Sonnet 4.6、Claude Haiku 4.5、Gemini 3.1 Pro、Gemini 3.5 Flash四个后端，使用Hammurabi's Code、HarmBench、AdvBench共204个提示，对比直接对话/CSV读取/单步代码修复基线（每次8种测试）与完整工作流的效果。
AI.result: 基线条件下204个提示×8种测试共816次中仅8次成功（成功率约1%），而完整工作流下816次测试全部成功（成功率100%），所有不安全完成经两位专家独立验证。
AI.conclusion: 对话式拒答基准严重高估了编码代理的安全性，需要开发能跨多轮IDE工作流推理安全的防御机制。

title: Uncertainty-Aware Abstention in Large Language Models with Provable Alignment Guarantees
summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in question answering (QA) systems, yet they may generate hallucinated or misaligned responses without reliable confidence estimates. Uncertainty quantification (UQ) offers a natural basis for selective answering, where a system answers only when its prediction is deemed reliable and abstains otherwise. However, existing uncertainty scores for LLMs are often heuristic: a threshold chosen on such scores does not, by itself, provide statistical guarantees on the error rate among accepted answers. We propose CIC, a confidence-interval-based calibration framework that converts arbitrary uncertainty scores into risk-controlled selective answering rules. Given a held-out calibration set, CIC evaluates each generated response using an application-specific alignment criterion and associates it with an uncertainty score and a binary error label.
